title: News report on climate policy
explanation:
News report on climate policy

Listen to a short news report.

Transcript

Reporter: Ministers have agreed to reduce national emissions by fifty per cent before 2035. The plan includes subsidies for electric vehicles, stricter rules for heavy industry and funding for home insulation. Environmental groups welcomed the targets but warned that enforcement will be critical. Opposition politicians argued the measures could raise energy costs for households in the short term.

questions.text: The plan aims to cut emissions by [*fifty|thirty|seventy] per cent.
Subsidies will support [*electric vehicles|air travel|coal mining].
Environmental groups welcomed the targets but stressed [*enforcement|advertising|tourism].
Opposition politicians fear higher [*energy costs|school fees|ticket prices].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The report mentions stricter rules for heavy ____.  | *industry | fashion | theatre
Funding will help improve home ____.  | *insulation | decoration | parking
Ministers have agreed on a new climate ____.  | *plan | holiday | concert
The deadline mentioned is the year ____.  | *2035 | 2020 | 2055
The report presents [*both support and criticism|only praise|only jokes].
The policy focuses on [*reducing emissions|increasing tourism|building stadiums].
Environmental groups think targets matter less than [*implementation|celebrities|sports].
